response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a strong generalist software engineer with 15+ years of experience, but he is a poor fit for this role on the two most critical hard requirements alone: he has no Japanese language proficiency whatsoever (the role requires native-level Japanese), and his career is firmly rooted in web, cloud, and application-layer development with TypeScript, Node.js, and React — not in C-based embedded firmware, Linux kernel modification, or network-security protocols. There is no evidence of experience with MACsec, IEEE 802.1X, Captive Portal, SNMP, NETCONF, LAN-switch internals, or any of the embedded networking security domains central to this position.
strengths: Over 15 years of broad engineering experience across embedded, systems, and application layers at reputable companies including Google and Meta
Some exposure to low-level C/C++ development (NI LabWindows components, wave field synthesis thesis using Linux and C++) and consistent Linux usage
Demonstrated ability to ramp quickly across diverse tech stacks and deliver in varied, complex domains
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency — the candidate lists only English, Romanian, and German, making this a hard disqualifier for a role requiring native-level Japanese
Zero security domain experience: no evidence of MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal, encryption key exchange, SNMP, NETCONF, or any network-security protocol work
No Linux kernel source modification, embedded firmware development, LAN-switch expertise, or chip-vendor SDK experience — core technical pillars of the role
